message:
feat(grpc): pluggable frame compression and real buffered streaming types

codec.rs: Replace hard-coded "compression not supported" error stubs with a
pluggable compression architecture using function pointer hooks:
- New type aliases `FrameCompressor` and `FrameDecompressor` for stateless
  frame-level compress/decompress functions
- Identity (passthrough) implementations for integration testing
- `FramedCodec` gains optional `compressor`/`decompressor` fields with builder
  methods `with_frame_codec()` and `with_identity_frame_codec()`
- Encode path now calls the configured compressor and sets the gRPC compressed
  flag byte; decode path calls the decompressor with max_message_size guard
- Manual Debug impl (function pointers don't derive Debug cleanly)
- New tests: identity codec roundtrip, custom decompressor size enforcement

streaming.rs: Replace pure `PhantomData` placeholder types with working
buffered-stream implementations:
- `StreamingRequest<T>` and `ResponseStream<T>` now back by `VecDeque` buffers
  with `closed` flag and `Waker` registration. `poll_next` returns Pending when
  open but empty (registering waker for push notification), Ready(None) only
  when closed and drained. New `open()`, `push()`, `close()` methods.
- `RequestSink<T>` gains `closed` flag and `sent_count` tracking. `send()`
  rejects after close with protocol error. `close()` changed from consuming
  `self` to `&mut self` for post-close inspection.
- New `NoopWaker` test helper for manually driving poll_next
- Three new lifecycle tests covering push/poll/close and post-close rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
